cards.text: Suaram: 6,892 SOSMA arrests in 5 years — up to 28% released without ever being charged.
cards.sub: Of arrests under the Security Offences (Special Measures) Act 2012 between 2018 and 2023, roughly one in four did not result in any charge being filed at all.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.h: SOSMA Section 4 allows 28 days of detention without charge since 2012 — replacing the ISA but retaining preventive-detention powers.
cards.s: The 28-day window can be used while authorities decide whether to charge. The 28% release-without-charge figure shows how often the answer is 'no'.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.h: SUHAKAM has run public inquiries into custodial conditions — most recently a 28-day inquiry into torture and death in custody at Taiping Prison.
cards.s: Custodial-death inquiries provide one of the few public windows into how preventive detention functions in practice.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.h: Suaram's annual reports remain the most consistent civil-society source on SOSMA usage — official Home Ministry figures are not released in disaggregated form.
cards.s: The asymmetry between civil-society reporting and government disclosure is itself a structural transparency gap.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.text: SOSMA is no longer a rare emergency tool — it is a routine pre-charge detention regime that releases one in four detainees uncharged.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.text: Like a 28-day holding cell where one in four occupants is released with no charge — and there is no public log of who entered or why.
cards.sub: Pre-charge detention used at scale, with limited public accountability for the cases that never produce charges.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.text: Genuine SOSMA reform requires disaggregated public reporting on arrests, durations, charges and outcomes — not just renewal debates every five years.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

issue_headline: Suaram: Up to 28% of SOSMA Detainees Released Without Charge, From Nearly 7,000 Arrests Since 2018
